rationale: This page provides a comprehensive overview of the Lokpal, including its statutory origin, tenure, eligibility criteria, and a detailed breakdown of its composition. It is a core topic for Polity and Governance.
cleanedText:
2. IMPORTANT CONSTITUTIONAL/ STATUTORY/ EXECUTIVE BODIES IN NEWS
2.1. LOKPAL
Why in the news?
Parliamentary Standing Committee on Personnel, Public Grievances, Law and Justice suggests urgent steps to operationalise Lokpal’s inquiry, prosecution wings in its 145th Report.
About Lokpal
- Genesis & Aim: Central, statutory, anti-corruption ombudsman established under the Lokpal & Lokayuktas Act, 2013 to investigate and prosecute corruption involving public servants.
- Recommended by 1st and 2nd Administrative Reforms Commissions.
- Lokayukta operates at State level; structure varies across States and is appointed by the Governor (generally in consultation with the State HC CJ and LoP).
- As per Lokpal and Lokayuktas Act 2013, power of Lokpal applies to all categories of public servants of India, including those serving under the Union Government, whether posted within India or abroad.
- First Lokpal: Justice Pinaki Chandra Ghosh (2019).
- Current Lokpal: Justice A.M. Khanwilkar.
- Tenure & Eligibility:
- Term: 5 years or till 70 years of age, whichever is earlier.
- No reappointment as Chairperson or Member after completion of term.
- A Member may become Chairperson only if total tenure does not exceed 5 years.
Lokpal Composition
- Chairperson: Serving or former CJI or Supreme Court Judge or an Eminent person.
- Eminent Person: With at least 25 years special knowledge and expertise in matters relating to anti-corruption policy, public administration, vigilance, finance including insurance and banking, law and management.
- Members: 8 members, with 50% judicial members. Not less than 50% of the Members shall be from amongst persons belonging to SC, STs, OBCS, Minorities and women.
- Eligibility of Judicial Member: He is or has been a Judge of Supreme Court or is or has been a Chief Justice of a High Court.
- Appointment: Appointed by the President on recommendations of a Selection Committee.
